subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Lost Child
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: It was the festival of spring. A small child was going to a fair with his parents. The road was full of people wearing bright clothes and walking happily toward the fair. The child felt excited and kept looking around at the lively surroundings.
questions: During which festival does the story begin?
Where was the child going with his parents?
How were the people dressed on the road?
How did the child feel during the journey?
What created a lively atmosphere on the road?

passages.paragraph: As they walked along, the child lagged behind his parents. He was fascinated by the toys displayed in the shops along the road. The bright colors and shapes attracted him greatly. However, his parents called him to move ahead and continue the journey.
questions: Why did the child lag behind his parents?
What attracted the child in the shops?
How were the toys described?
What did the parents ask the child to do?
Why did the child eventually move forward?

passages.paragraph: Soon they passed through fields full of blooming mustard flowers. The yellow flowers stretched across the fields, shining brightly under the sun. The child watched butterflies flying around and tried to catch them happily.
questions: What type of flowers were seen in the fields?
How were the mustard fields described?
What insects were flying around the flowers?
What did the child try to do with the butterflies?
How did the natural scene affect the child?

passages.paragraph: When they reached the fair, the child saw a sweet shop filled with delicious sweets. He especially wanted burfi and looked at his parents hopefully. However, knowing that his request might be refused, he moved on quietly.
questions: What type of shop did the child see at the fair?
Which sweet did the child want?
How did the child look at his parents?
Why did he not insist on getting the sweet?
What did the child do next?

passages.paragraph: As they walked further, the child saw a flower seller selling garlands of gulmohur flowers. The bright red flowers looked very attractive. The child wished to have a garland but again walked ahead without asking much.
questions: Who was selling flowers at the fair?
What type of flowers were sold?
How did the flowers look?
What did the child wish for?
Why did the child not insist on getting the flowers?

passages.paragraph: Next, the child noticed a balloon seller carrying many colorful balloons. The balloons floated in the air and looked very beautiful. The child wanted one but did not ask his parents because he knew they might refuse.
questions: What did the balloon seller carry?
How did the balloons appear?
What did the child want from the seller?
Why did the child not ask his parents?
What shows the child’s understanding of his parents?

passages.paragraph: Nearby, a snake-charmer was playing a flute to a dancing snake. The music was soft and attractive. Although the child wanted to stay and watch the performance, he remembered that his parents had warned him not to listen to such music.
questions: Who was playing the flute?
What animal was shown in the performance?
How was the music described?
Why did the child not stay to watch the show?
What does this show about the child?

passages.paragraph: Finally, the child reached a roundabout where many people were enjoying rides. He asked his parents for permission to ride it. When he turned to ask them, he suddenly realized that his parents were nowhere around him.
questions: What ride did the child see at the fair?
What did the child ask his parents?
What were people doing on the roundabout?
What realization shocked the child?
What caused the child’s fear?

passages.paragraph: The child began to cry loudly, calling for his mother and father. He ran here and there through the crowded fair searching for them. His heart was filled with fear and helplessness as he could not find them anywhere.
questions: What did the child do after realizing he was lost?
Whom did he call repeatedly?
Where did the child run while searching?
What emotions filled the child’s heart?
Why could he not find his parents?

passages.paragraph: A kind man in the crowd noticed the crying child and lifted him in his arms. He tried to comfort him by offering sweets, balloons, and a ride on the roundabout. However, the child refused everything and continued crying only for his parents.
questions: Who noticed the lost child in the crowd?
What did the man do to comfort him?
What attractions were offered to the child?
Did the child accept any of the offers?
Why did the child refuse everything?
